diagrams.title: Recursion Call Stack for Factorial(4)
diagrams.description: Shows how the call stack grows and shrinks during recursive factorial computation
diagrams.purpose: Understand how recursion uses stack memory and how return values propagate back

diagrams.title: Binary Search Recursive Call Tree
diagrams.description: Shows how binary search divides the array and narrows down the search space
elements: Initial call: search entire array [0 to n-1]
Mid element compared, determine which half to search
Recursive call on left or right half [low to mid-1] or [mid+1 to high]
Each level halves the search space
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the divide-and-conquer approach and understand O(log n) complexity

diagrams.title: Fibonacci Recursive Call Tree (Inefficient)
diagrams.description: Shows the redundant calculations in naive Fibonacci showing why it is exponential
elements: Tree has exponential number of nodes
diagrams.purpose: Understand why naive Fibonacci is O(2^n) and learn the need for memoization

diagrams.title: Tower of Hanoi - 3 Disks Solution
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of moving 3 disks from source to destination
elements: Step 1: Move disk 1 from A to C
Step 2: Move disk 2 from A to B
Step 3: Move disk 1 from C to B
Step 4: Move disk 3 from A to C (largest)
Step 5: Move disk 1 from B to A
Step 6: Move disk 2 from B to C
Step 7: Move disk 1 from A to C
diagrams.purpose: Understand the recursive pattern and why 2^n - 1 moves are needed
